_readme: Copy this file to ~/.config/memory-arbiter/config.json, then edit paths.
Why ~/.config/memory-arbiter/ instead of MCP client env or an install directory:
- MCP client env lives in each client config, so every client repeats the same vector settings.
- Client config and package directories may be rewritten during reinstall or migration.
- ~/.config/memory-arbiter/ is user-owned XDG config; pip/client installers do not overwrite it.
Keep per-client identity (MEMORY_ARBITER_CLIENT / AGENT_ID / WORKSPACE) in each MCP client's env block.
Do not put client/agent_id/workspace here unless you intentionally want one global default for every client.
Discovery order: MEMORY_ARBITER_CONFIG env > ~/.config/memory-arbiter/config.json > env/defaults.
First auto-embedding call loads the GGUF model lazily. A 300MB model may add visible startup latency once; later calls reuse the loaded model.
Section split — advanced feature for long-document retrieval (> threshold chars).
Splits long memories into sections with per-section vectors so memory_search can
return only the relevant section instead of the full text, saving LLM tokens.
Prerequisites (all required before enabled=true has any effect):
1. vec.enabled = true and sqlite-vec is installed
2. embedding.provider = gguf and model_path is valid
3. _vec_index_meta.state == ready (run memory_status to check)
How it works:
- memory_write saves content normally; if len(content) > threshold and all
prerequisites are met, the response includes a split_hint suggesting memory_split.
- memory_split(prepare) returns the full content in a single batch for an external
LLM to generate section boundaries (title/summary/anchor). v0.6.0 is single-batch;
for ultra-long docs, pre-chunk before memory_write or split across memories.
memory_split(publish) then validates offsets, generates per-section embeddings,
and atomically publishes.
- memory_search on a split memory returns matched_sections (title/summary only),
omitting full content. Agent calls get_sections for specific paragraphs or
memory_get for the full text.
section_vec_distance_threshold defaults to 0.42, calibrated on embeddinggemma-300m
(Q8): at 0.42, >=90% of relevant queries hit their target section while >=90% of
irrelevant sections are filtered. Re-calibrate if you switch embedding models
(run scripts/calibrate_section_threshold.py with your model and corpus).
All fields optional with defaults shown. Remove this block or leave enabled=false
to keep v0.5.4 behavior (no section splitting).
